# TMATMUL

<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_golden_manual.py; do not edit. -->


## Introduction

Matrix multiply (GEMM) producing an accumulator/output tile.

## Math Interpretation

Let:

- `M = aMatrix.GetValidRow()`
- `K = aMatrix.GetValidCol()`
- `N = bMatrix.GetValidCol()`

For `0 <= i < M` and `0 <= j < N` (output elements in the effective matmul domain):

$$ \mathrm{C}_{i,j} = \sum_{k=0}^{K-1} \mathrm{A}_{i,k} \cdot \mathrm{B}_{k,j} $$

Exact accumulator behavior and datatype promotion are target/implementation-defined.

## Assembly Syntax

PTO-AS form: see [PTO-AS Specification](assembly.md).

Synchronous form:

```text
%acc = tmatmul %a, %b : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
```

### AS Level 1 (SSA)

```text
%c = pto.tmatmul %a, %b : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
```

### AS Level 2 (DPS)

```text
pto.tmatmul ins(%a, %b : !pto.tile_buf<...>, !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%c : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```

### IR Level 1 (SSA)

```text
%c = pto.tmatmul %a, %b : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
```

### IR Level 2 (DPS)

```text
pto.tmatmul ins(%a, %b : !pto.tile_buf<...>, !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%c : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```
## C++ Intrinsic

C++ declaration:

```cpp
template <typename TileRes, typename TileLeft, typename SharedTileRight>
PTO_INST void TMATMUL(TileRes &cMatrix, TileLeft &aMatrix, SharedTileRight &bMatrix);
template <AccPhase Phase, typename TileRes, typename TileLeft, typename SharedTileRight>
PTO_INST void TMATMUL(TileRes &cMatrix, TileLeft &aMatrix, SharedTileRight &bMatrix);
```

## Constraints

- **Implementation checks (A2A3)**:
    - Supported `(CType, AType, BType)` triples:
    - `(int32_t, int8_t, int8_t)`
    - `(float, half, half)`
    - `(float, float, float)`
    - `(float, bfloat16_t, bfloat16_t)`
    - Runtime: `m/k/n` (taken from `aMatrix.GetValidRow()`, `aMatrix.GetValidCol()`, `bMatrix.GetValidCol()`) must be in `[1, 4095]`.
- **Implementation checks (A5)**:
    - Accumulator type must be `int32_t` or `float`.
    - If `int32_t`: `AType == int8_t` and `BType == int8_t`.
    - If `float`: supports `half/bfloat16_t/float` and selected fp8 pairs (target-defined).
    - Fractal/layout constraints are enforced:
    - Left: `Loc == Left`, `!isRowMajor`, `SFractal == RowMajor`
    - Acc: `Loc == Acc`, `!isRowMajor`, `SFractal == RowMajor`
    - Runtime: `m/k/n` (taken from `aMatrix.GetValidRow()`, `aMatrix.GetValidCol()`, `bMatrix.GetValidCol()`) must be in `[1, 4095]`.

## Examples

The right-hand matrix is a fully defined shared tile version. The four PEs
arrive independently, then each PE computes its M-axis quarter.

```cpp
SharedTile<half, K, N> shared_b;
if (get_thread_idx() == 0) {
  TLOAD(shared_b, global_b);
}
TMATMUL(c, a, shared_b);
```

The shared-right-hand-side form is normative for the group model. It is shown
as architecture-level C++ until compiler support lands.

## ASM Form Examples

### Auto Mode

```text
# Auto mode: compiler/runtime-managed placement and scheduling.
%c = pto.tmatmul %a, %b : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
```


### PTO Assembly Form

```text
%acc = tmatmul %a, %b : (!pto.tile<...>, !pto.tile<...>) -> !pto.tile<...>
# AS Level 2 (DPS)
pto.tmatmul ins(%a, %b : !pto.tile_buf<...>, !pto.tile_buf<...>) outs(%c : !pto.tile_buf<...>)
```

## Four-PE Group Contract

- The right operand MUST be a fully defined shared tile register version.
- The left and destination tiles are PE-local M-axis quarters of one logical
  tile.
- All four PEs execute the same dynamic matrix operation; they need not arrive
  in the same cycle.
- Tile-version dependencies provide readiness. No explicit synchronization
  token is part of the operation.
